text.title: Funnel Basics Examples Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Funnel Basics Examples` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each example MUST be tagged with its vertical (SaaS / DTC / mobile / B2B / subscription) and only matched against same-vertical funnels. Cross-vertical comparisons are flagged.
rule.rationale: Cross-vertical benchmarks lie (e-commerce checkout conversion ≠ SaaS signup); per-vertical tagging keeps the benchmarks honest.

rule.statement: Each example MUST cite baseline metric + after metric + duration + sample size + source. Hand-wave 'we improved' is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Cherry-picked anecdotes overstate lift; baseline+after+sample+source is the minimum credibility envelope.

rule.statement: Each example MUST surface the hypothesis (IF/THEN/BECAUSE) AND the tactic deployed. Without hypothesis, the example is just a tactic list.
rule.rationale: Tactic-only examples (do X) without the hypothesis lose the WHY; the hypothesis lets readers transfer the lesson, not just the tactic.

rule.statement: Each vertical MUST have a step-by-step benchmark table (median / top-quartile drop-off per step) with source + year. Stale benchmarks (> 24 months) are flagged.
rule.rationale: Funnel norms shift (iOS 14, cookieless, GDPR); stale benchmarks misdirect prioritisation.

rule.statement: The spec MUST name a single curator responsible for updating the examples + benchmark tables quarterly. Group handles forbidden.
rule.rationale: Reference data without an owner stales fast; named curator + quarterly review keeps it usable.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
